graphdb_rdf.metric_question: What is the floating point operations threshold and what is its notification procedure? Are there additional obligations for general-purpose AI models with systemic risk?
graphdb_rdf.metric_answer: ### Floating Point Operations Threshold The **floating point operations threshold** is a characteristic defined in the EU AI Act that relates to the classification of AI models. It is associated with requirements concerning industrial and technological changes. Specifically, it determines the classification of general-purpose AI models and is supplemented with indicators for model capability and benchmarks. For more details, you can refer to the following entity: - **[Floating Point Operations Threshold](https://euaiact.eu/resource/Characteristic/floating_point_operations_threshold)** ### Notification Procedure The specific notification procedure related to the floating point operations threshold is not detailed in the retrieved information. However, it is implied that compliance with this threshold is necessary for the classification and regulation of AI models. ### Obligations for General-Purpose AI Models with Systemic Risk General-purpose AI models that present systemic risks are subject to **relevant obligations** as outlined in the EU AI Act. This includes additional compliance requirements to ensure that these models are designed and operated in a manner that mitigates potential risks to society. For more information, you can explore the following entities: - **[General-Purpose AI Models with Systemic Risk](https://euaiact.eu/resource/AIModel/general-purpose_AI_models_with_systemic_risk)** - **[Relevant Obligations](https://euaiact.eu/resource/Obligation/relevant_obligations)** These obligations may include enhanced monitoring, risk assessment, and reporting requirements to ensure safety and compliance with regulatory standards.
